title: Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption:
Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?

This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.

The 2024 world map displays the status of mumps vaccines in national vaccination programs using a color-coded legend. Countries shaded in teal indicate that the vaccine is administered across the "Entire country." This category includes the United States, Canada, Brazil, Australia, Japan, and the majority of nations in Europe and South America. In contrast, countries shaded in reddish-orange represent regions where the vaccine is "Not routinely administered." This grouping covers the vast majority of the African continent, including Nigeria, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, as well as India, China, and Indonesia. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, corresponding to the "No data" category in the legend. The "Regions of the country" category, represented by a tan color, does not appear to be applied to any visible landmass on this map. The distribution shows a distinct geographic pattern where routine administration is prevalent in the Americas and Europe, while large portions of Africa and Asia fall into the non-routine category. The metadata notes that improved mumps vaccines became available for the first time in 1967, providing historical context for the current global coverage levels depicted.
